titel: The Swedish Migration Agency's participation during Almedalen Week 2026.
html:
2026-06-22

The Swedish Migration Agency will participate in Almedalsveckan (Almedal Week) in Visby. During the week, the agency will host two of its own seminars, participate in several external events, and meet key stakeholders for discussions on current issues related to migration and societal development.

During Almedalsveckan, the Swedish Migration Agency will be involved in a total of 14 program segments. A particular focus this year is the implementation of the EU's migration and asylum pact. Other relevant topics on the agenda include: preparedness of authorities, labor market exploitation, artificial intelligence (AI), and LGBTQI+ issues.

The Swedish Migration Agency's programs have been developed jointly by the Executive Staff and the Communications Department. According to Anna Lindblad, Chief of Staff at the Executive Staff, participating in Almedalsveckan is an important part of the agency's mission.

"As a specialized authority, it is important that we participate in the public discussion about migration. There are extensive changes taking place in the field of migration, not least through the EU's migration and asylum pact. Therefore, we need to contribute our knowledge, experience, and perspective to the issues being discussed," said Anna Lindblad, Chief of Staff at the Executive Staff.

Here are the program segments in which the Swedish Migration Agency will participate:

Tuesday 13:30–14:30

How does the EU's migration and asylum pact affect the reception of refugees in Sweden? Link to external website.

Organizer: The Swedish Migration Agency Location: S:t Hansgatan 21, Almedalsarenan

Participants: Veronika Lindstrand Kant, Director responsible for the implementation of the EU's migration and asylum pact, The Swedish Migration Agency Johanna Ó Duinnín, Case Officer, The Police Authority Karin Ödquist Drackner, Migration expert and policy advisor, The Swedish Red Cross Maria Mindhammar, Director General and opening speaker, The Swedish Migration Agency Jesper Tengroth, Press Chief and moderator, The Swedish Migration Agency

Tuesday 15:00–16:00

How can different societal actors work together on voluntary return? Link to external website.

Organizer: The Swedish Migration Agency and the National Coordinator for Voluntary Return

Location: S:t Hansgatan 21, Almedal Arena

Participants:

Teresa Zetterblad, National Coordinator for Voluntary Return Petra Lindh, Head of Unit and Expert, The Swedish Migration Agency Bilal Almobarak, CEO, Support Group Network Åsa Simonsson, Municipal Director, Svalöv Municipality Katarina Gentzel Sandberg, Moderator, Gullers Group

Other seminars where the Swedish Migration Agency is represented:

Tuesday 14:00–14:30

Mandatory but flexible – how does Sweden's solidarity model affect the country? Link to external website.

Delegation for Migration Studies (Delmi) Location: Hamnplan, space 221 Participants: Jenny Bergsten, Investigation Secretary, Delegation for Migration Studies Bernd Parusel, Senior Researcher in Political Science, Swedish Institute for European Policy Studies Charlotte Marie, Section Chief, The Swedish Migration Agency, Region West

Tuesday 17:00–18:00

Leading in critical situations – are government employers prepared for heightened readiness? Link to external website.

Organizer: The Swedish Government Employers' Association Location: Korsgatan 4, County Administrative Board garden Participants: Charlotte Petri Gornitzka, Governor, Gotland County Maria Mindhammar, Director General, The Swedish Migration Agency Johan Kuylenstierna, Director General, the Swedish Environmental Protection Agency Susanne Thedéen, National Antiquities Inspector, the Swedish National Heritage Board Robert Egnell, Rector, the Swedish Defence University Jens Mattsson, Director General, FOI (Swedish Defence Research Institute) Ann Lindberg, Director General, the Swedish Medical Products Agency Johan Norrman, Director General, the Swedish Customs Service Ann Follin, Director General, The Swedish Government Employers' Association

Wednesday 10:45–11:45

From initiative to accelerated capability – how does the public sector build an AI strategy that lasts? Link to external website.

Organizer: CGI Location: Birgers gränd 7, garden with entrance from Almedalen Participants:

Cecilia Elb, Vice President of Business Consulting, CGI Christian Dahlgren, Director of Consulting and Infrastructure Architect, CGI Ingrid Halvorsen, Head of Digitalization, Varberg Municipality Johan Magnusson, Professor, University of Gothenburg and UTTC Stefan Andersson, Section Chief, Swedish Migration Agency

Wednesday 9:00 AM – 10:15 AM

Is workplace crime Sweden's most underestimated problem? Who controls the Swedish labor market? [Link to external website]

Organizers: Södertörn University and "Sweden Against Organized Crime" (SMOB) Location: Skeppsbron 24, Joda Bar & Kitchen

Participants: Maria Mindhammar, Director General, Swedish Migration Agency Jonas Andé, Expert on workplace crime in Sweden, Skanska Sverige Birgitta Pettersson, Municipal Director, Uppsala Municipality Tomas Andersson, National Coordinator for combating workplace crime, Swedish Authority for Financial Crime Carina Lindfelt, Head of Department for Labor Market and Working Environment, Confederation of Swedish Enterprise Mattias Schulstad, Investigator, Social Policy Unit, LO (Swedish Trade Union Confederation) Lars Lööw, Director General, Swedish Work Environment Authority

Thursday 11:15 AM – 11:45 AM

Asylum applications from individuals citing sexual orientation and gender identity/expression as grounds for asylum. [Link to external website]

Delegation for Migration Studies (Delmi) Location: Hamnplan, location 221

Participants: Anna Hammarstedt, Investigation Secretary, Delegation for Migration Studies Lovise Brade, President of RFSL (Swedish Federation for LGBTQ Rights) Anna Lindblad, Head of the Authority Staff, Swedish Migration Agency
tldr: The Swedish Migration Agency will participate in the Almedalen Week in 2026 to discuss new regulations within the EU and address topics such as refugee reception, technology, and security.
lattlast:
The Swedish Migration Agency is a government agency responsible for matters related to migration. During Almedalsveckan (Almedal Week) in Visby, they will participate in 14 different meetings and events. The purpose is to share knowledge about how new regulations from the EU affect Sweden and how to best manage refugee reception.

During the week, experts from the Migration Agency will discuss several important topics. These include how artificial intelligence can be used in society, how work is done with individuals who wish to return to their home countries, and how government agencies should prepare for crises.

The Migration Agency will also participate in discussions on other important issues, including crime in workplaces, and how the asylum process works for individuals seeking protection based on their sexual orientation or gender identity.
